channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: About 4 in 10 worried about environmental impacts of AI: Survey
item.description: A new poll shows growing concerns among citizens over the potential environmental effects caused by artificial intelligence. The Associated Press-NORC Center poll, released Thursday, found about 4 in 10 U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Scientists spot skyscraper-sized asteroid racing through solar system
item.description: Astronomers have discovered asteroid 2025 SC79, a skyscraper-sized space rock orbiting the sun in just 128 days. the second-fastest known.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration pushes to get AI data centers on the grid faster
item.description: The Trump administration issued a proposal late Thursday that aims to help AI data centers get connected to the electric grid more quickly.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: Intel sees supply shortage, will prioritize data center technology
item.description: Intel reported better than expected Q3 figures, reflecting a turnaround in sales along with cutting operational expenses and a renewed focus on its core business. On the downside, Intel is enjoying a little too much success and is seeing a shortage of supply.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In the summers, the sky is jet black when Raul Cruz arrives at this Imperial Valley sugarcane field to start his day. He chops, cleans and bundles the crop, taking heed as the sun rises. It's hard work, but so is starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and JAE C. HONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: OpenAI seeks to automate ‘computer use’ for Macs in the enterprise
item.description: While AI bots have begun mastering tasks in browsers and on Windows, Mac-using enterprises have largely been overlooked, until now. OpenAI aims to change that with its acquisition of generative AI interface maker Software Applications Incorporated.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI is connecting all company secrets to ChatGPT - all your work data and apps are now open
item.description: Company knowledge, a self-explanatory ChatGPT tool, will cite internal documents for accurate reporting.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: Some of the top Sims 4 creators have just quit the game’s EA Creator Network, citing the ongoing EA sale to Saudi-backed investors – here’s what lilsimsie, James Turner and more have said in their statements
item.description: Here's what some of the top Sims 4 creators have to say on the proposed EA sale deal, including info on who's leaving the EA Creator Network.
item.dc:creator: Jake Green, TechRadar

item.title: AI ‘workslop’: The new productivity killer only training can stop
item.description: AI offers workers the promise of increased efficiency and productivity, freeing them up from repetitive work to tackle more complex tasks. But as companies have rolled out AI tools to employees, many are facing a different challenge: AI-generated work that does the opposite.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows 95 icons still exist in Windows 11 today as dusty old relics from another time – here's where to find them
item.description: Nostalgic for Windows 95? Believe it or not, you can still find icons from the dusty old OS kicking about in Windows 11.
item.dc:creator: Darren Allan, TechRadar

item.title: Turning vision into value: The strategic role of intelligent IP cameras
item.description: The cameras and sensors that are installed across your business are doing more than watching. These IP cameras serve as a network of intelligent eyes across your operations, providing a potentially untapped resource that could improve business intelligence and operational efficiencies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Next.js 16 features explicit caching, AI-powered debugging
item.description: Vercel’s React framework for building full-stack web applications gets an upgrade in Next.js 16, with more explicit caching and AI-based debugging, among other features. Announced October 21, Next.js 16 now is generally available, with installation instructions available at nextjs.org.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 5 big updates coming to the ChatGPT Atlas browser
item.description: OpenAI is rushing out quality-of-life upgrades for ChatGPT Atlas that promise to make the AI-powered browser better.
item.dc:creator: Eric Hal Schwartz, TechRadar

item.title: Krafton shifting to 'AI-first' game development
item.description: The makers of popular video game "PUBG: Battlegrounds" are switching gears and becoming an "AI-first" developer.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: From friendly text to financial trap: the new scam trend
item.description: Friendly text conversations about BBQs and social events can lead to WEEX gold trading scams that target older adults with fake investment opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inside Google’s multi-architecture revolution: Axion Arm joins x86 in production clusters
item.description: While x86 has been dominant for decades, a new migration project at Google represents a significant shift to more mixed architectures.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Reddit sues Perplexity and others for allegedly scraping millions of user comments
item.description: Social media platform Reddit sued the artificial intelligence company Perplexity AI and three other entities on Wednesday, alleging their involvement in an “industrial-scale, unlawful” economy to “scrape” the comments of millions of Reddit users for commercial gain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: PyTorch team unveils framework for programming clusters
item.description: The PyTorch team at Meta, stewards of the PyTorch open source machine learning framework, has unveiled Monarch, a distributed programming framework intended to bring the simplicity of PyTorch to entire clusters.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Solarium commission urges reversal on Trump’s cyber cuts
item.description: Cuts at CISA, the State Department and elsewhere have stalled, and in some cases reversed, progress on the Cyberspace Solarium Commission's recommendations. The post Solarium commission urges reversal on Trump’s cyber cuts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon hopes robots can replace 600K future hires: Report
item.description: Amazon hopes robots can replace hundreds of thousands of future roles, according to leaked documents seen by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: AI helps IT teams resolve incidents faster, report finds
item.description: New data suggests that generative artificial intelligence (genAI) is delivering measurable benefits for IT organizations and their IT Service Management (ITSM) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta cutting 600 AI jobs even as it continues to hire more for its superintelligence lab
item.description: MENLO PARK, Calif. (AP) — Meta Platforms is cutting roughly 600 artificial intelligence jobs even as it continues to hire more workers for its superintelligence lab, the company confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kandji becomes Iru, opens MDM for Windows and Android
item.description: Apple device management and security company Kandji has changed its name to Iru, reflecting a new approach to what it does while opening its offer up to Windows and Android.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ukraine unveils upgraded sea drone it says can strike anywhere in the Black Sea
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's state security service has unveiled an upgraded sea drone it says can now operate anywhere in the Black Sea, carry heavier weapons and use artificial intelligence for targeting.
item.dc:creator: EFREM LUKATSKY and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Google and Apple face extra UK scrutiny over 'strategic' role in mobile platforms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s antitrust watchdog on Wednesday targeted Google and Apple for their “strategic” roles in mobile ecosystems, opening the door for regulators to impose changes to their business practices to improve competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida test scores rise, attendance improves after cellphone ban
item.description: A new study finds that Florida test scores rose and attendance improved after a school cellphone ban, although short-term disciplinary problems also arose in the state.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Storage constraints add to AI data center bottleneck
item.description: After GPUs, storage capacity has emerged as the next major constraint for AI data centers. Hard-drive lead times are ballooning to more than a year, and enterprise flash storage is also expected to see shortages and price increases, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Call to ban AI superintelligence could redraw the global tech race between the US and China
item.description: More than 850 prominent figures have called for a prohibition on developing AI superintelligence, a move that could reshape enterprise AI investments and intensify the US-China technology race if adopted.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use Python dataclasses
item.description: Everything in Python is an object, or so the saying goes. If you want to create your own custom objects, with their own properties and methods, you use Python’s class object to do it.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Netflix blames tax dispute in Brazil for rare quarterly earnings letdown
item.description: Netflix missed the earnings target set by stock market analysts during the video streamer’s latest quarter, a letdown that the company blamed on a tax dispute in Brazil. The results announced Tuesday broke Netflix's six-quarter streak of posting a profit that eclipsed analysts' projections.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OpenAI launches web browser ChatGPT Atlas: How to start using it
item.description: OpenAI announced Tuesday it is launching a ChatGPT-powered web browser called Atlas that will compete directly with widely used Google Chrome.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: OPM renews effort to consolidate 119 HR systems into one
item.description: The Office of Personnel Management is looking to implement a commercial software-as-a-service human capital management system for all agencies by July 4, 2027. The post OPM renews effort to consolidate 119 HR systems into one first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: AI dominates Gartner’s top strategic technology trends for 2026
item.description: To no one’s surprise, AI took center stage at the Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo going on this week in Orlando, Fla., where the research firm made its annual presentation of the core technology trends set to shape enterprise IT in the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The lost 30 years: The decline of Japan’s semiconductor industry
item.description: Global industrial competition has entered an era in which “whoever controls semiconductors controls the future.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Unlocking efficiency with AI, commercial software and the cloud
item.description: Government agencies are already exploring opportunities to apply artificial intelligence to their operations in ways that increase efficiency and save money. The post Unlocking efficiency with AI, commercial software and the cloud first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Teens face new PG-13 limits on Instagram
item.description: New Instagram parental controls allow families to manage teen screen time and content limits through the Family Center with stricter safety settings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
